matches.match_rationale: Enrollment intake follows seasonal, cohort-based, and application-stage time patterns that are ideal for time-series forecasting. A model trained on historical application volumes, conversion rates, and intake cycles can generate rolling projections that feed directly into capacity planning — giving operations teams advance visibility before decisions are locked. This directly addresses the core failure: operations discovering projected intake only after enrollment decisions are made.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An enrollment forecasting engine that ingests application pipeline data and outputs weekly/monthly intake projections by site and program, with confidence intervals — automatically pushed to operations dashboards so capacity planning can begin 3-6 months ahead.

matches.match_rationale: The core dysfunction is a disconnection between enrollment projections and capacity constraints — classrooms, staff headcount, and facilities are hired or allocated without feedback from actual intake numbers. Resource allocation optimization directly models this: given projected enrollment (input), assign available capacity (classrooms, teachers, support staff) across sites and cohorts under defined constraints. This turns a reactive, siloed process into a coordinated planning loop.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A capacity coordination tool that takes enrollment forecasts as input and optimally assigns teaching staff, classrooms, and support resources across campuses — surfacing shortfalls or surpluses 60-90 days before term start so hiring and facility decisions can be made proactively.

matches.match_rationale: Aligning enrollment targets with capacity across multiple sites involves satisfying simultaneous hard constraints: maximum class sizes, minimum staff-to-student ratios, licensed facility capacities, and teacher certifications per program type. Constraint satisfaction finds valid configurations across these dimensions that time-series forecasting or resource scheduling alone cannot enforce simultaneously. This is especially relevant for multi-site operators where constraints differ by campus.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A scenario planning tool where enrollment teams can input projected intake numbers and see which site/program combinations are feasible given current capacity constraints — flagging conflicts before commitments are made to applicants or staff.

matches.match_rationale: The feedback loop between enrollment and operations is currently manual and episodic — operations learns of intake projections too late because there is no automated handoff. A task automation agent can monitor enrollment pipeline milestones (application counts, acceptance rates, deposit confirmations) and automatically trigger capacity planning workflows: notifying operations leads, updating shared capacity trackers, and escalating when projected intake approaches or exceeds site thresholds. This automates the coordination process rather than just improving the forecasts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An enrollment-to-operations relay agent that watches CRM/SIS data for intake signals, updates a shared capacity dashboard in real time, and fires off templated alerts to operations managers when intake projections cross predefined thresholds for any site.

matches.match_rationale: Operations teams and leadership need narrative capacity planning reports that translate forecast numbers into actionable staffing and facility decisions. Text generation can produce interpretive summaries — e.g., 'Site B is projected to be over capacity by 12% in Q3; recommend hiring 2 additional teachers and securing one overflow classroom' — reducing the translation burden on enrollment managers who currently lack time to communicate projections in operational terms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Automated capacity planning briefings generated each week from enrollment forecast data: plain-language summaries per site with staffing implications, flagged risks, and recommended actions — distributed to operations leads without manual report writing.
